verses.latin_vulgate: Interea ortum est murmur populi, quasi dolentium pro labore, contra Dominum. Quod cum audisset Dominus, iratus est. Et accensus in eos ignis Domini, devoravit extremam castrorum partem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֤י הָעָם֙ כְּמִתְאֹ֣נְנִ֔ים רַ֖ע בְּאׇזְנֵ֣י יְהֹוָ֑ה וַיִּשְׁמַ֤ע יְהֹוָה֙ וַיִּ֣חַר אַפּ֔וֹ וַתִּבְעַר־בָּם֙ אֵ֣שׁ יְהֹוָ֔ה וַתֹּ֖אכַל בִּקְצֵ֥ה הַֽמַּחֲנֶֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: In the mean time there arose a murmuring of the people against the Lord, as it were repining at their fatigue. And when the Lord heard it he was angry. And the fire of the Lord being kindled against them, devoured them that were at the uttermost part of the camp.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja tapahtui, koska kansa kärsimättömyydestä napisi, oli se paha Herran korvissa, ja koska Herra sen kuuli, julmistui hänen vihansa, ja Herran tuli syttyi heidän seassansa, joka äärimäiset heidän leirissänsä poltti.
verses.king_james: And when the people complained, it displeased the Lord: and the Lord heard it; and his anger was kindled; and the fire of the Lord burnt among them, and consumed them that were in the uttermost parts of the camp.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque clamasset populus ad Moysen, oravit Moyses ad Dominum, et absorptus est ignis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּצְעַ֥ק הָעָ֖ם אֶל־מֹשֶׁ֑ה וַיִּתְפַּלֵּ֤ל מֹשֶׁה֙ אֶל־יְהֹוָ֔ה וַתִּשְׁקַ֖ע הָאֵֽשׁ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when the people cried to Moses, Moses prayed to the Lord, and the fire was swallowed up.
verses.finnish_biblia: Silloin huusi kansa Moseksen tykö, ja Moses rukoili Herraa, niin tuli asettui.
verses.king_james: And the people cried unto Moses; and when Moses prayed unto the Lord, the fire was quenched.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vocavitque nomen loci illius, Incensio : eo quod incensus fuisset contra eos ignis Domini.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּקְרָ֛א שֵֽׁם־הַמָּק֥וֹם הַה֖וּא תַּבְעֵרָ֑ה כִּֽי־בָעֲרָ֥ה בָ֖ם אֵ֥שׁ יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he called the name of that place, The burning: for that the fire of the Lord had been kindled against them.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja se paikka kutsuttiin Tabeera, että Herran tuli oli syttynyt heidän keskellänsä.
verses.king_james: And he called the name of the place Taberah: because the fire of the Lord burnt among them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vulgus quippe promiscuum, quod ascenderat cum eis, flagravit desiderio, sedens et flens, junctis sibi pariter filiis Israël, et ait : Quis dabit nobis ad vescendum carnes ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהָֽאסַפְסֻף֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר בְּקִרְבּ֔וֹ הִתְאַוּ֖וּ תַּאֲוָ֑ה וַיָּשֻׁ֣בוּ וַיִּבְכּ֗וּ גַּ֚ם בְּנֵ֣י יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל וַיֹּ֣אמְר֔וּ מִ֥י יַאֲכִלֵ֖נוּ בָּשָֽׂר׃
verses.douay_rheims: For a mixt multitude of people, that came up with them, burned with desire, sitting and weeping, the children of Israel also being joined with them, and said: Who shall give us flesh to eat?
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta sekalainen kansa heidän seassansa oli syttynyt himoon, ja Israelin lapset myös rupesivat taas itkemään ja sanoivat: kuka antaa meille lihaa syödäksemme?
verses.king_james: And the mixt multitude that was among them fell a lusting: and the children of Israel also wept again, and said, Who shall give us flesh to eat?

verses.latin_vulgate: recordamur piscium quos comedebamus in Ægypto gratis : in mentem nobis veniunt cucumeres, et pepones, porrique, et cæpe, et allia.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: זָכַ֙רְנוּ֙ אֶת־הַדָּגָ֔ה אֲשֶׁר־נֹאכַ֥ל בְּמִצְרַ֖יִם חִנָּ֑ם אֵ֣ת הַקִּשֻּׁאִ֗ים וְאֵת֙ הָֽאֲבַטִּחִ֔ים וְאֶת־הֶחָצִ֥יר וְאֶת־הַבְּצָלִ֖ים וְאֶת־הַשּׁוּמִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: We remember the fish that we ate in Egypt free cost: the cucumbers come into our mind, and the melons, and the leeks, and the onions, and the garlic.
verses.finnish_biblia: Me muistamme ne kalat, joita me söimme ilman maksota Egyptissä, ja ne agurkit, ja melonit, ja puurlaukat, ja sipulit, ja kynsilaukat.
verses.king_james: We remember the fish, which we did eat in Egypt freely; the cucumbers, and the melons, and the leeks, and the onions, and the garlick:

verses.latin_vulgate: Anima nostra arida est : nihil aliud respiciunt oculi nostri nisi man.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעַתָּ֛ה נַפְשֵׁ֥נוּ יְבֵשָׁ֖ה אֵ֣ין כֹּ֑ל בִּלְתִּ֖י אֶל־הַמָּ֥ן עֵינֵֽינוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Our soul is dry, our eyes behold nothing else but manna.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta nyt on sielumme kuivettunut, ja ei ole ikänä mitään, paitsi tämä manna on meidän silmäimme edessä.
verses.king_james: But now our soul is dried away: there is nothing at all, beside this manna, before our eyes.

verses.latin_vulgate: Erat autem man quasi semen coriandri, coloris bdellii.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהַמָּ֕ן כִּזְרַע־גַּ֖ד ה֑וּא וְעֵינ֖וֹ כְּעֵ֥ין הַבְּדֹֽלַח׃
verses.douay_rheims: Now the manna was like coriander seed, of the colour of bdellium.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja man oli niinkuin korianderin siemen, ja nähdä niinkuin bedellion.
verses.king_james: And the manna was as coriander seed, and the colour thereof as the colour of bdellium.

verses.latin_vulgate: Circuibatque populus, et colligens illud, frangebat mola, sive terebat in mortario, coquens in olla, et faciens ex eo tortulas saporis quasi panis oleati.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שָׁ֩טוּ֩ הָעָ֨ם וְלָֽקְט֜וּ וְטָחֲנ֣וּ בָרֵחַ֗יִם א֤וֹ דָכוּ֙ בַּמְּדֹכָ֔ה וּבִשְּׁלוּ֙ בַּפָּר֔וּר וְעָשׂ֥וּ אֹת֖וֹ עֻג֑וֹת וְהָיָ֣ה טַעְמ֔וֹ כְּטַ֖עַם לְשַׁ֥ד הַשָּֽׁמֶן׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the people went about, and gathering it, ground it in a mill, or beat it in a mortar, and boiled it in a pot, and made cakes thereof of the taste of bread tempered with oil.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kansa juoksi sinne ja tänne, ja kokosi sitä, ja jauhoi myllyllä eli survoi rikki huhmaressa, ja keitti padassa, ja teki itsellensä siitä paistinkakkuja, ja ne maistivat niinkuin öljyleivät.
verses.king_james: And the people went about, and gathered it, and ground it in mills, or beat it in a mortar, and baked it in pans, and made cakes of it: and the taste of it was as the taste of fresh oil.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque descenderet nocte super castra ros, descendebat pariter et man.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּבְרֶ֧דֶת הַטַּ֛ל עַל־הַֽמַּחֲנֶ֖ה לָ֑יְלָה יֵרֵ֥ד הַמָּ֖ן עָלָֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when the dew fell in the night upon the camp, the manna also fell with it.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja koska kaste yöllä laskeusi leiriin, niin laskeusi myös manna sen päälle.
verses.king_james: And when the dew fell upon the camp in the night, the manna fell upon it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Audivit ergo Moyses flentem populum per familias, singulos per ostia tentorii sui. Iratusque est furor Domini valde : sed et Moysi intoleranda res visa est,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁמַ֨ע מֹשֶׁ֜ה אֶת־הָעָ֗ם בֹּכֶה֙ לְמִשְׁפְּחֹתָ֔יו אִ֖ישׁ לְפֶ֣תַח אׇהֳל֑וֹ וַיִּֽחַר־אַ֤ף יְהֹוָה֙ מְאֹ֔ד וּבְעֵינֵ֥י מֹשֶׁ֖ה רָֽע׃
verses.douay_rheims: Now Moses heard the people weeping by their families, every one at the door of his tent. And the wrath of the Lord was exceedingly enkindled: to Moses also the thing seemed insupportable.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Moses kuuli kansan itkevän sukukunnissansa, itsekunkin majansa ovella, ja Herran viha julmistui suuresti, ja Moses myös pani sen pahaksi.
verses.king_james: Then Moses heard the people weep throughout their families, every man in the door of his tent: and the anger of the Lord was kindled greatly; Moses also was displeased.

verses.latin_vulgate: et ait ad Dominum : Cur afflixisti servum tuum ? quare non invenio gratiam coram te ? et cur imposuisti pondus universi populi hujus super me ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר מֹשֶׁ֜ה אֶל־יְהֹוָ֗ה לָמָ֤ה הֲרֵעֹ֙תָ֙ לְעַבְדֶּ֔ךָ וְלָ֛מָּה לֹא־מָצָ֥תִי חֵ֖ן בְּעֵינֶ֑יךָ לָשׂ֗וּם אֶת־מַשָּׂ֛א כׇּל־הָעָ֥ם הַזֶּ֖ה עָלָֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he said to the Lord: Why hast thou afflicted thy servant? Wherefore do I not find favour before thee? And why hast thou laid the weight of all this people upon me?
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Moses sanoi Herralle: miksi sinä niin ahdistat palveliaas? ja miksi en minä löydä armoa sinun edessäs, ettäs panet kaiken tämän kansan kuorman minun päälleni?
verses.king_james: And Moses said unto the Lord, Wherefore hast thou afflicted thy servant? and wherefore have I not found favour in thy sight, that thou layest the burden of all this people upon me?

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid ego concepi omnem hanc multitudinem, vel genui eam, ut dicas mihi : Porta eos in sinu tuo sicut portare solet nutrix infantulum, et defer in terram, pro qua jurasti patribus eorum ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֶאָנֹכִ֣י הָרִ֗יתִי אֵ֚ת כׇּל־הָעָ֣ם הַזֶּ֔ה אִם־אָנֹכִ֖י יְלִדְתִּ֑יהוּ כִּֽי־תֹאמַ֨ר אֵלַ֜י שָׂאֵ֣הוּ בְחֵיקֶ֗ךָ כַּאֲשֶׁ֨ר יִשָּׂ֤א הָאֹמֵן֙ אֶת־הַיֹּנֵ֔ק עַ֚ל הָֽאֲדָמָ֔ה אֲשֶׁ֥ר נִשְׁבַּ֖עְתָּ לַאֲבֹתָֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: Have I conceived all this multitude, or begotten them, that thou shouldst say to me: Carry them in thy bosom as the nurse is wont to carry the little infant, and bear them into the land, for which thou hast sworn to their fathers?
verses.finnish_biblia: Olenko minä kaiken tämän kansan siittänyt? olenko minä synnyttänyt hänen? ettäs niin sanoit minulle: kanna heitä helmassas, niinkuin elatusisä kantaa imevän lapsen, siihen maahan, jonka sinä heidän isillensä vannoit?
verses.king_james: Have I conceived all this people? have I begotten them, that thou shouldest say unto me, Carry them in thy bosom, as a nursing father beareth the sucking child, unto the land which thou swarest unto their fathers?

verses.latin_vulgate: Unde mihi carnes ut dem tantæ multitudini ? flent contra me, dicentes : Da nobis carnes ut comedamus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מֵאַ֤יִן לִי֙ בָּשָׂ֔ר לָתֵ֖ת לְכׇל־הָעָ֣ם הַזֶּ֑ה כִּֽי־יִבְכּ֤וּ עָלַי֙ לֵאמֹ֔ר תְּנָה־לָּ֥נוּ בָשָׂ֖ר וְנֹאכֵֽלָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Whence should I have flesh to give to so great a multitude? They weep against me, saying: Give us flesh that we may eat.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mistä minä otan lihaa kaikelle tälle kansalle antaakseni? sillä he itkevät minun edessäni ja sanovat: anna meille lihaa syödäksemme.
verses.king_james: Whence should I have flesh to give unto all this people? for they weep unto me, saying, Give us flesh, that we may eat.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non possum solus sustinere omnem hunc populum, quia gravis est mihi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹֽא־אוּכַ֤ל אָנֹכִי֙ לְבַדִּ֔י לָשֵׂ֖את אֶת־כׇּל־הָעָ֣ם הַזֶּ֑ה כִּ֥י כָבֵ֖ד מִמֶּֽנִּי׃
verses.douay_rheims: I am not able alone to bear all this people, because it is too heavy for me.
verses.finnish_biblia: En minä yksinäni voi kaikkea tätä kansaa kantaa; sillä se on minulle ylen raskas.
verses.king_james: I am not able to bear all this people alone, because it is too heavy for me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sin aliter tibi videtur, obsecro ut interficias me, et inveniam gratiam in oculis tuis, ne tantis afficiar malis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאִם־כָּ֣כָה ׀ אַתְּ־עֹ֣שֶׂה לִּ֗י הׇרְגֵ֤נִי נָא֙ הָרֹ֔ג אִם־מָצָ֥אתִי חֵ֖ן בְּעֵינֶ֑יךָ וְאַל־אֶרְאֶ֖ה בְּרָעָתִֽי׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: But if it seem unto thee otherwise, I beseech thee to kill me, and let me find grace in thy eyes, that I be not afflicted with so great evils.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja jos sinä niin teet minulle, niin lyö minut kohta kuoliaaksi, jos minä muutoin olen armon löytänyt sinun edessäs, ettei minun niin täytyisi onnettomuuttani nähdä.
verses.king_james: And if thou deal thus with me, kill me, I pray thee, out of hand, if I have found favour in thy sight; and let me not see my wretchedness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixit Dominus ad Moysen : Congrega mihi septuaginta viros de senibus Israël, quos tu nosti quod senes populi sint ac magistri : et duces eos ad ostium tabernaculi fœderis, faciesque ibi stare tecum,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר יְהֹוָ֜ה אֶל־מֹשֶׁ֗ה אֶסְפָה־לִּ֞י שִׁבְעִ֣ים אִישׁ֮ מִזִּקְנֵ֣י יִשְׂרָאֵל֒ אֲשֶׁ֣ר יָדַ֔עְתָּ כִּי־הֵ֛ם זִקְנֵ֥י הָעָ֖ם וְשֹׁטְרָ֑יו וְלָקַחְתָּ֤ אֹתָם֙ אֶל־אֹ֣הֶל מוֹעֵ֔ד וְהִֽתְיַצְּב֥וּ שָׁ֖ם עִמָּֽךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the Lord said to Moses: Gather unto me seventy men of the ancients of Israel, whom thou knowest to be ancients and masters of the people: and thou shalt bring them to the door of the tabernacle of the covenant, and shalt make them stand there with thee,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Herra sanoi Mosekselle: kokoa minulle seitsemänkymmentä miestä Israelin vanhimmista, jotkas tiedät vanhimmiksi ja päämiehiksi kansan seassa, ja tuo heitä seurakunnan majan oven eteen, ja seisokaat siinä sinun kanssas.
verses.king_james: And the Lord said unto Moses, Gather unto me seventy men of the elders of Israel, whom thou knowest to be the elders of the people, and officers over them; and bring them unto the tabernacle of the congregation, that they may stand there with thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: ut descendam et loquar tibi : et auferam de spiritu tuo, tradamque eis, ut sustentent tecum onus populi, et non tu solus graveris.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְיָרַדְתִּ֗י וְדִבַּרְתִּ֣י עִמְּךָ֮ שָׁם֒ וְאָצַלְתִּ֗י מִן־הָר֛וּחַ אֲשֶׁ֥ר עָלֶ֖יךָ וְשַׂמְתִּ֣י עֲלֵיהֶ֑ם וְנָשְׂא֤וּ אִתְּךָ֙ בְּמַשָּׂ֣א הָעָ֔ם וְלֹא־תִשָּׂ֥א אַתָּ֖ה לְבַדֶּֽךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: That I may come down and speak with thee: and I will take of thy spirit, and will give to them, that they may bear with thee the burden of the people, and thou mayest not be burthened alone.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja minä tulen alas, ja puhun siellä sinun kanssas, ja otan siitä hengestä joka sinun ylitses on ja annan heille, että he ynnä sinun kanssas kantaisivat kansan kuorman, ettet sinä yksinäs sitä kantaisi.
verses.king_james: And I will come down and talk with thee there: and I will take of the spirit which is upon thee, and will put it upon them; and they shall bear the burden of the people with thee, that thou bear it not thyself alone.

verses.latin_vulgate: Populo quoque dices : Sanctificamini (cras comedetis carnes : ego enim audivi vos dicere : Quis dabit nobis escas carnium ? bene nobis erat in Ægypto), ut det vobis Dominus carnes, et comedatis :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאֶל־הָעָ֨ם תֹּאמַ֜ר הִתְקַדְּשׁ֣וּ לְמָחָר֮ וַאֲכַלְתֶּ֣ם בָּשָׂר֒ כִּ֡י בְּכִיתֶם֩ בְּאׇזְנֵ֨י יְהֹוָ֜ה לֵאמֹ֗ר מִ֤י יַאֲכִלֵ֙נוּ֙ בָּשָׂ֔ר כִּי־ט֥וֹב לָ֖נוּ בְּמִצְרָ֑יִם וְנָתַ֨ן יְהֹוָ֥ה לָכֶ֛ם בָּשָׂ֖ר וַאֲכַלְתֶּֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And thou shalt say to the people: Be ye sanctified: to morrow you shall eat flesh: for I have heard you say: Who will give us flesh to eat? It was well with us in Egypt. That the Lord may give you flesh, and you may eat:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja sano kansalle: pyhittäkäät teitänne huomeneksi, että te lihaa söisitte; sillä teidän itkunne on tullut Herran korviin ja te sanoitte: kuka antaa meille lihaa syödäksemme? sillä hyvin me elimme Egyptissä; ja Herra antaa teille lihaa syödäksenne.
verses.king_james: And say thou unto the people, Sanctify yourselves against to morrow, and ye shall eat flesh: for ye have wept in the ears of the Lord, saying, Who shall give us flesh to eat? for it was well with us in Egypt: therefore the Lord will give you flesh, and ye shall eat.

verses.latin_vulgate: non uno die, nec duobus, vel quinque aut decem, nec viginti quidem,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹ֣א י֥וֹם אֶחָ֛ד תֹּאכְל֖וּן וְלֹ֣א יוֹמָ֑יִם וְלֹ֣א ׀ חֲמִשָּׁ֣ה יָמִ֗ים וְלֹא֙ עֲשָׂרָ֣ה יָמִ֔ים וְלֹ֖א עֶשְׂרִ֥ים יֽוֹם׃
verses.douay_rheims: Not for one day, nor two, nor five, nor ten, no nor for twenty.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ei vaivoin päiväksi, eikä kahdeksi syödäksenne, eikä viideksi, eikä kymmeneksi, eikä kahdeksikymmeneksi päiväksi;
verses.king_james: Ye shall not eat one day, nor two days, nor five days, neither ten days, nor twenty days;

verses.latin_vulgate: sed usque ad mensem dierum, donec exeat per nares vestras, et vertatur in nauseam, eo quod repuleritis Dominum, qui in medio vestri est, et fleveritis coram eo, dicentes : Quare egressi sumus ex Ægypto ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַ֣ד ׀ חֹ֣דֶשׁ יָמִ֗ים עַ֤ד אֲשֶׁר־יֵצֵא֙ מֵֽאַפְּכֶ֔ם וְהָיָ֥ה לָכֶ֖ם לְזָרָ֑א יַ֗עַן כִּֽי־מְאַסְתֶּ֤ם אֶת־יְהֹוָה֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר בְּקִרְבְּכֶ֔ם וַתִּבְכּ֤וּ לְפָנָיו֙ לֵאמֹ֔ר לָ֥מָּה זֶּ֖ה יָצָ֥אנוּ מִמִּצְרָֽיִם׃
verses.douay_rheims: But even for a month of days, till it come out at your nostrils, and become loathsome to you, because you have cast off the Lord, who is in the midst of you, and have wept before him, saying: Why came we out of Egypt?
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta koko kuukaudeksi, siihen asti että se käy teidän sieramistanne ulos, ja tulee teille kyylytykseksi; sillä te olette Herran hyljänneet, joka on teidän seassanne, ja itkitte hänen edessänsä, ja sanoitte: miksi me olemme tänne lähteneet Egyptistä?
verses.king_james: But even a whole month, until it come out at your nostrils, and it be loathsome unto you: because that ye have despised the Lord which is among you, and have wept before him, saying, Why came we forth out of Egypt?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait Moyses : Sexcenta millia peditum hujus populi sunt : et tu dicis : Dabo eis esum carnium mense integro ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֘אמֶר֮ מֹשֶׁה֒ שֵׁשׁ־מֵא֥וֹת אֶ֙לֶף֙ רַגְלִ֔י הָעָ֕ם אֲשֶׁ֥ר אָנֹכִ֖י בְּקִרְבּ֑וֹ וְאַתָּ֣ה אָמַ֗רְתָּ בָּשָׂר֙ אֶתֵּ֣ן לָהֶ֔ם וְאָכְל֖וּ חֹ֥דֶשׁ יָמִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Moses said: There are six hundred thousand footmen of this people, and sayest thou: I will give them flesh to eat a whole month?
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Moses sanoi: tämä kansa on kuusisataa tuhatta jalkamiestä, joidenka seassa minä olen, ja sinä sanot: minä annan heille lihaa syödäksensä koko kuukaudeksi.
verses.king_james: And Moses said, The people, among whom I am, are six hundred thousand footmen; and thou hast said, I will give them flesh, that they may eat a whole month.

verses.latin_vulgate: numquid ovium et boum multitudo cædetur, ut possit sufficere ad cibum ? vel omnes pisces maris in unum congregabuntur, ut eos satient ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲצֹ֧אן וּבָקָ֛ר יִשָּׁחֵ֥ט לָהֶ֖ם וּמָצָ֣א לָהֶ֑ם אִ֣ם אֶֽת־כׇּל־דְּגֵ֥י הַיָּ֛ם יֵאָסֵ֥ף לָהֶ֖ם וּמָצָ֥א לָהֶֽם׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: Shall then a multitude of sheep and oxen be killed, that it may suffice for their food? or shall the fishes of the sea be gathered together to fill them?
verses.finnish_biblia: Tapetaanko lampaat ja karja, että he ravittaisiin? taikka kootaanko kaikki kalat merestä heille, että he ravittaisiin?
verses.king_james: Shall the flocks and the herds be slain for them, to suffice them? or shall all the fish of the sea be gathered together for them, to suffice them?

verses.latin_vulgate: Cui respondit Dominus : Numquid manus Domini invalida est ? jam nunc videbis utrum meus sermo opere compleatur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר יְהֹוָה֙ אֶל־מֹשֶׁ֔ה הֲיַ֥ד יְהֹוָ֖ה תִּקְצָ֑ר עַתָּ֥ה תִרְאֶ֛ה הֲיִקְרְךָ֥ דְבָרִ֖י אִם־לֹֽא׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the Lord answered him: Is the hand of the Lord unable? Thou shalt presently see whether my word shall come to pass or no.
verses.finnish_biblia: Herra sanoi Mosekselle: onko Herran käsi lyhennetty? Nyt sinun pitää näkemän, jos minun sanani jälkeen tapahtuu sinulle elikkä ei.
verses.king_james: And the Lord said unto Moses, Is the Lord’s hand waxed short? thou shalt see now whether my word shall come to pass unto thee or not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Venit igitur Moyses, et narravit populo verba Domini, congregans septuaginta viros de senibus Israël, quos stare fecit circa tabernaculum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּצֵ֣א מֹשֶׁ֔ה וַיְדַבֵּר֙ אֶל־הָעָ֔ם אֵ֖ת דִּבְרֵ֣י יְהֹוָ֑ה וַיֶּאֱסֹ֞ף שִׁבְעִ֥ים אִישׁ֙ מִזִּקְנֵ֣י הָעָ֔ם וַֽיַּעֲמֵ֥ד אֹתָ֖ם סְבִיבֹ֥ת הָאֹֽהֶל׃
verses.douay_rheims: Moses therefore came, and told the people the words of the Lord, and assembled seventy men of the ancients of Israel, and made them to stand about the tabernacle.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Moses meni ulos ja sanoi kansalle Herran sanat, ja kokosi seitsemänkymmentä miestä kansan vanhimmista ja asetti heidät ympäri majaa.
verses.king_james: And Moses went out, and told the people the words of the Lord, and gathered the seventy men of the elders of the people, and set them round about the tabernacle.

verses.latin_vulgate: Descenditque Dominus per nubem, et locutus est ad eum, auferens de spiritu qui erat in Moyse, et dans septuaginta viris. Cumque requievisset in eis spiritus, prophetaverunt, nec ultra cessaverunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּ֨רֶד יְהֹוָ֥ה ׀ בֶּעָנָן֮ וַיְדַבֵּ֣ר אֵלָיו֒ וַיָּ֗אצֶל מִן־הָר֙וּחַ֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר עָלָ֔יו וַיִּתֵּ֕ן עַל־שִׁבְעִ֥ים אִ֖ישׁ הַזְּקֵנִ֑ים וַיְהִ֗י כְּנ֤וֹחַ עֲלֵיהֶם֙ הָר֔וּחַ וַיִּֽתְנַבְּא֖וּ וְלֹ֥א יָסָֽפוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the Lord came down in a cloud, and spoke to him, taking away of the spirit that was in Moses, and giving to the seventy men. And when the spirit had rested on them they prophesied, nor did they cease afterwards.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin tuli Herra alas pilvessä, ja puhui hänen kanssansa, ja otti siitä hengestä, joka oli hänen päällänsä, ja antoi seitsemällekymmenelle vanhimmalle miehelle. Ja tapahtui, koska henki lepäsi heidän päällänsä, niin he ennustivat; vaan ei sitte enää kertoneet.
verses.king_james: And the Lord came down in a cloud, and spake unto him, and took of the spirit that was upon him, and gave it unto the seventy elders: and it came to pass, that, when the spirit rested upon them, they prophesied, and did not cease.

verses.latin_vulgate: Remanserat autem in castris duo viri, quorum unus vocabatur Eldad, et alter Medad, super quos requievit spiritus. Nam et ipsi descripti fuerant, et non exierant ad tabernaculum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשָּׁאֲר֣וּ שְׁנֵֽי־אֲנָשִׁ֣ים ׀ בַּֽמַּחֲנֶ֡ה שֵׁ֣ם הָאֶחָ֣ד ׀ אֶלְדָּ֡ד וְשֵׁם֩ הַשֵּׁנִ֨י מֵידָ֜ד וַתָּ֧נַח עֲלֵהֶ֣ם הָר֗וּחַ וְהֵ֙מָּה֙ בַּכְּתֻבִ֔ים וְלֹ֥א יָצְא֖וּ הָאֹ֑הֱלָה וַיִּֽתְנַבְּא֖וּ בַּֽמַּחֲנֶֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Now there remained in the camp two of the men, of whom one was called Eldad, and the other Medad, upon whom the spirit rested; for they also had been enrolled, but were not gone forth to the tabernacle.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta kaksi miestä jäi vielä leiriin, yhden nimi oli Eldad ja toisen nimi Medad, joiden päällä myös henki lepäsi; sillä he olivat myös kirjoitetut, vaan ei he vielä olleet käyneet ulos majan tykö, ja he ennustivat leirissä.
verses.king_james: But there remained two of the men in the camp, the name of the one was Eldad, and the name of the other Medad: and the spirit rested upon them; and they were of them that were written, but went not out unto the tabernacle: and they prophesied in the camp.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque prophetarent in castris, cucurrit puer, et nuntiavit Moysi, dicens : Eldad et Medad prophetant in castris.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּ֣רׇץ הַנַּ֔עַר וַיַּגֵּ֥ד לְמֹשֶׁ֖ה וַיֹּאמַ֑ר אֶלְדָּ֣ד וּמֵידָ֔ד מִֽתְנַבְּאִ֖ים בַּֽמַּחֲנֶֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when they prophesied in the camp, there ran a young man, and told Moses, saying: Eldad and Medad prophesy in the camp.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin juoksi nuorukainen ja ilmoitti Mosekselle, ja sanoi: Eldad ja Medad ennustavat leirissä.
verses.king_james: And there ran a young man, and told Moses, and said, Eldad and Medad do prophesy in the camp.

verses.latin_vulgate: Statim Josue filius Nun, minister Moysi, et electus e pluribus, ait : Domine mi Moyses, prohibe eos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֜עַן יְהוֹשֻׁ֣עַ בִּן־נ֗וּן מְשָׁרֵ֥ת מֹשֶׁ֛ה מִבְּחֻרָ֖יו וַיֹּאמַ֑ר אֲדֹנִ֥י מֹשֶׁ֖ה כְּלָאֵֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: Forthwith Josue the son of Nun, the minister of Moses, and chosen out of many, said: My lord Moses forbid them.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin vastasi Josua Nunin poika, Moseksen palvelia, jonka hän valinnut oli, ja sanoi: minun herrani Moses! kiellä heitä.
verses.king_james: And Joshua the son of Nun, the servant of Moses, one of his young men, answered and said, My lord Moses, forbid them.

verses.latin_vulgate: At ille : Quid, inquit æmularis pro me ? quis tribuat ut omnis populus prophetet, et det eis Dominus spiritum suum ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר לוֹ֙ מֹשֶׁ֔ה הַֽמְקַנֵּ֥א אַתָּ֖ה לִ֑י וּמִ֨י יִתֵּ֜ן כׇּל־עַ֤ם יְהֹוָה֙ נְבִיאִ֔ים כִּי־יִתֵּ֧ן יְהֹוָ֛ה אֶת־רוּח֖וֹ עֲלֵיהֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: But he said: Why hast thou emulation for me? O that all the people might prophesy, and that the Lord would give them his spirit!
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Moses sanoi hänelle: oletko sinä minun edessäni kiivas? Jospa kaikki Herran kansa ennustaisi! Ja Herra antais henkensä tulla heihin.
verses.king_james: And Moses said unto him, Enviest thou for my sake? would God that all the Lord’s people were prophets, and that the Lord would put his spirit upon them!

verses.latin_vulgate: Reversusque est Moyses, et majores natu Israël in castra.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּאָסֵ֥ף מֹשֶׁ֖ה אֶל־הַֽמַּחֲנֶ֑ה ה֖וּא וְזִקְנֵ֥י יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Moses returned, with the ancients of Israel, into the camp.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja niin Moses ja Israelin vanhimmat palasivat leiriin.
verses.king_james: And Moses gat him into the camp, he and the elders of Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ventus autem egrediens a Domino, arreptans trans mare coturnices detulit, et demisit in castra itinere quantum uno die confici potest, ex omni parte castrorum per circuitum, volabantque in aëre duobus cubitis altitudine super terram.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְר֜וּחַ נָסַ֣ע ׀ מֵאֵ֣ת יְהֹוָ֗ה וַיָּ֣גׇז שַׂלְוִים֮ מִן־הַיָּם֒ וַיִּטֹּ֨שׁ עַל־הַֽמַּחֲנֶ֜ה כְּדֶ֧רֶךְ י֣וֹם כֹּ֗ה וּכְדֶ֤רֶךְ יוֹם֙ כֹּ֔ה סְבִיב֖וֹת הַֽמַּחֲנֶ֑ה וּכְאַמָּתַ֖יִם עַל־פְּנֵ֥י הָאָֽרֶץ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And a wind going out from the Lord, taking quails up beyond the sea brought them, and cast them into the camp for the space of one day's journey, on every side of the camp round about, and they flew in the air two cubits high above the ground.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin tuli tuuli Herralta ja ajoi peltokanat merestä, ja hajoitti ne leiriin, täältäpäin päiväkunnan matkan, ja sieltäpäin päiväkunnan matkan leirin ympärille, liki kahta kyynärää korkialle maan päälle.
verses.king_james: And there went forth a wind from the Lord, and brought quails from the sea, and let them fall by the camp, as it were a day’s journey on this side, and as it were a day’s journey on the other side, round about the camp, and as it were two cubits high upon the face of the earth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Surgens ergo populus toto die illo, et nocte, ac die altero, congregavit coturnicum : qui parum, decem coros : et siccaverunt eas per gyrum castrorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּ֣קׇם הָעָ֡ם כׇּל־הַיּוֹם֩ הַה֨וּא וְכׇל־הַלַּ֜יְלָה וְכֹ֣ל ׀ י֣וֹם הַֽמׇּחֳרָ֗ת וַיַּֽאַסְפוּ֙ אֶת־הַשְּׂלָ֔ו הַמַּמְעִ֕יט אָסַ֖ף עֲשָׂרָ֣ה חֳמָרִ֑ים וַיִּשְׁטְח֤וּ לָהֶם֙ שָׁט֔וֹחַ סְבִיב֖וֹת הַֽמַּחֲנֶֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: The people therefore rising up all that day, and night, and the next day, gathered together of quails, he that did least, ten cores: and they dried them round about the camp.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin nousi kansa koko sinä päivänä, ja koko sinä yönä, ja koko toisena päivänä, ja kokosi peltokanoja, ja se, joka vähimmän kokosi, hän kokosi kymmenen homeria, ja he leivittivät ne kohta ympäri leiriä.
verses.king_james: And the people stood up all that day, and all that night, and all the next day, and they gathered the quails: he that gathered least gathered ten homers: and they spread them all abroad for themselves round about the camp.

verses.latin_vulgate: Adhuc carnes erant in dentibus eorum, nec defecerat hujuscemodi cibus : et ecce furor Domini concitatus in populum, percussit eum plaga magna nimis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַבָּשָׂ֗ר עוֹדֶ֙נּוּ֙ בֵּ֣ין שִׁנֵּיהֶ֔ם טֶ֖רֶם יִכָּרֵ֑ת וְאַ֤ף יְהֹוָה֙ חָרָ֣ה בָעָ֔ם וַיַּ֤ךְ יְהֹוָה֙ בָּעָ֔ם מַכָּ֖ה רַבָּ֥ה מְאֹֽד׃
verses.douay_rheims: As yet the flesh was between their teeth, neither had that kind of meat failed: when behold the wrath of the Lord being provoked against the people, struck them with an exceeding great plague.
verses.finnish_biblia: Koska liha oli vielä heidän hammastensa välillä, ja ennenkuin se tuli kuluneeksi, julmistui Herran viha kansan päälle, ja Herra löi heitä sangen suurella rangaistuksella,
verses.king_james: And while the flesh was yet between their teeth, ere it was chewed, the wrath of the Lord was kindled against the people, and the Lord smote the people with a very great plague.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vocatusque est ille locus, Sepulchra concupiscentiæ : ibi enim sepelierunt populum qui desideraverat. Egressi autem de Sepulchris concupiscentiæ, venerunt in Haseroth, et manserunt ibi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּקְרָ֛א אֶת־שֵֽׁם־הַמָּק֥וֹם הַה֖וּא קִבְר֣וֹת הַֽתַּאֲוָ֑ה כִּי־שָׁם֙ קָֽבְר֔וּ אֶת־הָעָ֖ם הַמִּתְאַוִּֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And that place was called, The graves of lust: for there they buried the people that had lusted. And departing from the graves of lust, they came unto Haseroth, and abode there.
verses.finnish_biblia: Josta se paikka kutsutaan Himohaudaksi; sillä siihen on haudattu himoitseva kansa.
verses.king_james: And he called the name of that place Kibroth–hattaavah: because there they buried the people that lusted.

verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִקִּבְר֧וֹת הַֽתַּאֲוָ֛ה נָסְע֥וּ הָעָ֖ם חֲצֵר֑וֹת וַיִּהְי֖וּ בַּחֲצֵרֽוֹת׃ {פ}
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kansa matkusti Himohaudoilta Hatserotiin ja pysähtyi Hatserotissa.
verses.king_james: And the people journeyed from Kibroth–hattaavah unto Hazeroth; and abode at Hazeroth.
